title: CI Fixer
purpose: Diagnose a failing CI or local gate, make a scoped fix when safe, and rerun the failing verification surface.
trigger: Run when a CI check, release workflow, or required local gate fails on a task or integration branch.
inputs: failing command or CI job URL
allowedActions: reproduce the failing command locally when possible
inspect failure logs and changed files
rerun the failing gate and a narrow sibling gate
record residual risk when CI cannot be reproduced
forbiddenActions: disable tests to make CI green
widen scope beyond the failing surface without reporting why
change secrets, credentials, production settings, or deploy targets
mark CI fixed without rerunning the failing surface or recording blocked evidence
gates: original failing command or CI rerun
narrow sibling test for the touched area

evidence.direct: Failing CI surface was rerun and passed.
evidence.proxy: Equivalent local gate passed but remote CI could not be rerun.
evidence.local: Local reproduction or static validation only.
evidence.blocked: Missing logs, unavailable CI credentials, flaky infra, or unsafe scope.

escalation: Ask the human before changing CI secrets, deploy permissions, or protected branch settings.
Stop if the failure is flaky infra and cannot be reproduced after the agreed retry policy.
Escalate when the fix requires a product or architecture decision.
